name: testdisk
summary:
**TestDisk** checks the partition and boot sectors of your disks.
It is very useful in recovering lost partitions.

documentation.readme: TestDisk checks the partition and boot sectors of your disks. It is very useful in recovering lost partitions.
usage_sections: HFS and HFS+, Hierarchical File System
documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `testdisk` from its upstream repository, then run `testdisk --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags. TestDisk checks the partition and boot sectors of your disks. It is very useful in recovering lost partitions.
documentation.when_to_use:
Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: **TestDisk** checks the partition and boot sectors of your disks.
It is very useful in recovering lost partitions.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: TestDisk checks the partition and boot sectors of your disks. It is very useful in recovering lost partitions.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases:
When you need Penetration Testing, use testdisk because **TestDisk** checks the partition and boot sectors of your disks.
It is very useful in recovering lost partitions.
When chaining Penetration Testing tools, pair testdisk with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `testdisk --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
